commands.review_summary:
The branch restores selected legacy channel/helper exports from the deprecated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/compat` subpath and adds focused compat export coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main publishes the compat subpath but does not export the legacy helper names restored by this PR, so those named imports would fail. I did not run an executable repro because the checkout had to remain read-only.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the PR now has real behavior proof, is reported mergeable, and the review found no concrete code defect.

target.title: plugin-sdk: restore legacy compat helper exports
target.body:
## Summary
- extend the deprecated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/compat` surface with additional legacy channel plugin helpers
- keep the root `openclaw/plugin-sdk` export small; this only affects the explicit compat subpath
- add coverage that imports the restored helpers from `compat`

## Tests
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/compat.test.ts`
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
- `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs`

## External import proof
From a temp directory outside the repo, symlinked the checked-out package as `node_modules/openclaw` and imported the public package subpath:

```console
$ node --input-type=module <<'JS'
import {
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  buildRandomTempFilePath,
  extractToolSend,
  resolveThreadSessionKeys,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/compat";

const extracted = extractToolSend({ action: "sendMessage", to: "chat", threadId: 42 });
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  buildRandomTempFilePath: typeof buildRandomTempFilePath,
  resolveThreadSessionKeys: typeof resolveThreadSessionKeys,
  extracted,
}));
JS
{"DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID":"default","buildRandomTempFilePath":"function","resolveThreadSessionKeys":"function","extracted":{"to":"chat","threadId":"42"}}
(node:3636646) [OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_SDK_COMPAT_DEPRECATED] Warning: openclaw/plugin-sdk/compat is deprecated for new plugins. Migrate to focused openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath> imports. See https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/sdk-migration
Bundled plugins must use scoped plugin-sdk subpaths. External plugins may keep compat temporarily while migrating. Migration guide: https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/sdk-migration
```
target.author: san-tian
